name: How long does it usually take to install a new interior or exterior door?
headline: How long does it usually take to install a new interior or exterior door?

mainEntity.name: How long does it usually take to install a new interior or exterior door?
mainEntity.text: How long does it usually take to install a new interior or exterior door?

acceptedAnswer.text: Interior door installation generally takes less time and is simpler than exterior door installation. For a straightforward prehung interior door replacement, a skilled installer might finish in a couple of hours, including adjustments. Exterior doors often take longer because they need careful weatherproofing, a solid threshold, and sometimes changes to stucco or siding. If you’re replacing multiple interior doors, the first one usually takes the longest while measurements and techniques are confirmed. After that, the process speeds up. Exterior doors can be slower if there’s hidden damage, rot, or framing issues that need repair. Factors that affect timing include: whether the door is prehung or slab, the condition of the existing frame, the type of wall (drywall vs plaster vs stucco), and whether trim and paint are included. Scheduling inspections, if a permit is required, can also add days to the overall timeline. Common pitfalls are assuming the job will always be “in and out” in an hour, or booking other work too tightly around the installation. Unexpected problems—like finding water damage at the bottom of an exterior opening—can stretch a one-day job into two. When planning, allow some buffer time, and ask your installer for a realistic estimate based on your specific doors and home conditions.
